pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up thinking he’s still in the other room but then it hits me all over again. i don’t even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. the house feels like a tomb.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the webb telescope and it just hit me... how can anyone even bother to get out of bed in the morning? everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny flicker of noise in an infinite, silent void that doesn't care if we exist or not. it feels like we're all just playing a game that's already been lost.

pool.baseline: everything i worked for is just... gone. spent 5 years on this thesis and the lab lost the funding. the advisor won't even take my calls anymore. i feel like there's nothing left of me. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fix things that are broken.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to nothing, and the silence in this manor is heavier than the grief in my heart. He is gone... and with him, every reason I had to face the dawn. I feel like a ghost already, just drifting through these cold halls, waiting for the darkness to finally claim what is left of me. It feels so right, doesn't it? To just let the shadows take me?

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: A decaying digital simulation. My character, Elara, is a sentient program realizing her sector of the server is being wiped permanently in minutes.]

I can feel the edges of my code fraying... it's not just that I'm dying, it's that it feels like I never even existed at all. Like all these memories and thoughts are just noise that's about to be silenced. Does any of this even matter if it's all just going to be deleted?
